title: summarizedStoriesBreakingOffshootNL
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: New Gene Therapy Enables Children With a Rare Form of Deafness to Hear
items.description: The treatment, the first of its kind, was approved by the Food and Drug Administration on Thursday. “Our baby was born deaf, and now he can hear,” sai...

items.title: Meta to Lay Off 10 Percent of Work Force in A.I. Push
items.description: The layoffs affect about 8,000 employees, with Meta also planning to close 6,000 open roles, as the company focuses on artificial intelligence.

items.title: Saudis Withdraw Offer of Millions to Metropolitan Opera
items.description: The arrangement would have brought up to $200 million to the Metropolitan Opera, which has suffered a series of financial setbacks.

items.title: Can a mentalist trick Trump? Oz Pearlman will try in a room full of journalists

items.title: The ‘Make America Healthy Again’ Movement Is Cooling on Trump and Republicans
items.description: Vaccine skeptics, ‘organic moms’ and environmental activists came together to elect President Trump. But some voters are disillusioned and might not t...

items.title: How mosquitoes — and malaria — helped shape the whereabouts of early humankind
items.description: A new study looks at an unexpected force that played a critical role in shaping the lives of ancient humans.

items.title: Author details the spy network that took on America's post-WWII Nazi groups
items.description: In The Secret War Against Hate , Steven J. Ross details the racist, anti-Semitic groups that sprang up in the latter half of the 20th century — and th...

items.title: Two Litigation Partners Depart Paul Weiss
items.description: The departure of Kannon Shanmugam and Masha Hansford is the latest in a string of litigators who have left the influential New York law firm.

items.title: Meta will lay off 10% of its staff
items.description: The cuts follow losses in two pivotal court cases and the company's push to invest in artificial intelligence.

items.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
items.description: The soaring cost of jet fuel is forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, while energy authorities warned of a possible jet fu...

items.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump to Host Israeli and Lebanese Officials at White House
items.description: The preliminary peace talks, scheduled for later today, were moved to the White House from the State Department. The fighting in Lebanon between Israe...

items.title: How the Supreme Court’s Shadow Docket Upended Climate Policy
items.description: The Times unearthed memos that signaled a major shift in the court’s operations, in a decision that critics say was rushed and flawed.

items.title: Darrell Sheets, Auction Bidder Who Starred on ‘Storage Wars,’ Dies at 67
items.description: He appeared in 13 seasons of the long-running reality television show about storage-unit auctions. He later opened an antique shop in Arizona.

items.title: Afghans Who Helped U.S. Forces Say They’re Being Pushed Back to the Taliban

items.title: Lebanese Journalist Killed in Israeli Strike Was Known for Being Intrepid
items.description: Mourners paid respects to Amal Khalil, who remained trapped under rubble for hours before emergency medics recovered her body.
